# `prom_api` golden fixture provenance

Issue #32 architect plan amendment §3 (pinned mechanism): these fixtures are
captured **once, by a human**, from a real Prometheus instance, via the
committed [`capture.sh`](capture.sh) script — never regenerated by CI.
`pulsus-server`'s wire-format tests (`src/prom_api/encode.rs`) assert our own
encoder's output against these files byte-for-byte (for the deterministic
fixtures — see the caveat below).

## Source

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Image | `prom/prometheus:v3.13.0` |
| Image digest (pinned, `capture.sh` resolves by this, not the tag) | `sha256:0e698e35e50d1ddc2d11a4a55b089fe62eb71358a5c204dfafd21bdf8ffe04b8` |
| Prometheus version (from the capture's own `status/buildinfo`) | `3.13.0`, revision `40af9c2cdc0eda00f3622e867a27f6359f7295f3` |
| Capture date | 2026-07-14 (re-run same day for the code-review round-1 fixture additions below) |
| Capture host | podman 3.4.4, rootless |

## Seed data

`capture.sh` writes a small OpenMetrics fixture (embedded in the script) and
imports it via `promtool tsdb create-blocks-from --experimental openmetrics`
— every seeded sample lands at the **fixed** reference instant
`1435781451` (`2015-07-01T20:10:51Z`, Prometheus's own documented API
example timestamp), so the query/query_range/labels/label_values/series
fixtures below are reproducible independent of wall-clock capture time:

```
up{job="node",instance="localhost:9100"} 1 1435781451.000
up{job="node",instance="localhost:9101"} 0 1435781451.000
http_requests_total{job="api",method="get",code="200"} 1027 1435781451.000
http_requests_total{job="api",method="post",code="500"} 3 1435781451.000
```

The container also runs its own default self-scrape (`job="prometheus"`,
`2s` interval, deliberately fast for this one-shot capture rather than
Prometheus's own `15s` default) so `/api/v1/metadata` and `/api/v1/status/
tsdb` have real, non-empty scrape-derived content — the metadata content and
the HEAD block's series/timestamps that depends on are legitimately
capture-time-relative (the caveat below).

## Deviation this capture proved (see issue #32 implementation notes)

The architect plan amendment §2 pinned `prom_float`'s mechanism as Go
`strconv.FormatFloat(v,'g',-1,64)` (an `%e`/`%f` threshold at `exp < -4 ||
exp >= 21`). **This capture disproves that assumption**: real
`prom/prometheus:v3.13.0` formats every finite value via
`strconv.FormatFloat(v,'f',-1,64)` — always fixed-point, never scientific
notation, at any magnitude (see `query.scalar_1e21.json`:
`"1000000000000000000000"`, not `"1e+21"`; `query.scalar_5e_minus_324.json`;
`query.scalar_f64_max.json`). `pulsus-server`'s `prom_float` was corrected
to match this captured evidence — see that function's own doc comment in
`src/prom_api/encode.rs`.

## Code-review round-1 fixture additions

- `labels.matcher_only_get.json`, `series.matcher_only_get.json`,
  `label_values.matcher_only_get.json` — `match[]={job="node"}` (a
  matcher-only selector, no concrete metric name), added to lock the
  code-review round-1 fix: Prometheus's own `match[]` contract permits
  this, and the fixtures prove it matches only the seeded `up` series
  (`job="node"`), never `http_requests_total` (`job="api"`).
- `metadata.derived_suffix_name_get.json` — `?metric=go_goroutines_bucket`
  returns `{"status":"success","data":{}}`: real Prometheus never
  fabricates a match for a derived-series-suffixed name that isn't itself
  a registered family, confirming `MetricsEngine::metadata` must not
  strip suffixes (code-review round-1 finding, disposition: not a defect,
  kept verbatim).

## `status/tsdb`'s `numSamples` (code-review round-1 fix)

The original implementation queried `SELECT count() FROM metric_samples`
for `numSamples`, violating the zero-ClickHouse `status/tsdb` contract
(task-manager resolution #2 on issue #32) — and `numSamples` was never a
real Prometheus `headStats` field in the first place (real `headStats` is
`numSeries`/`numLabelPairs`/`chunkCount`/`minTime`/`maxTime`, see
`status.tsdb_get.json` below). `numSamples` has been **removed**:
`status/tsdb` now reads only `numSeries`/`seriesCountByMetricName` from
the resident label-cache snapshot, no ClickHouse query at all. See
docs/api.md §3.4.

## Determinism caveat

`status.runtimeinfo_get.json` and `status.tsdb_get.json` are **not**
byte-reproducible across capture runs: real Prometheus's `runtimeinfo`
reports its own process `startTime`/`serverTime` (wall clock at capture),
and `status/tsdb`'s `headStats` reflects only the **in-memory head block**
(minutes-old data at capture time — our imported 2015-dated block is
already compacted to on-disk storage and therefore invisible to `headStats`,
a genuine Prometheus behavior, not a capture bug) plus the self-scrape's own
churn. `pulsus-server`'s wire-format tests therefore assert these two
fixtures' **envelope shape** (the documented `data.headStats.{numSeries,
numSamples}`/`data.seriesCountByMetricName` sub-shape we implement, and
`data.{startTime,storageRetention}`) rather than byte-exact equality —
PulsusDB's own `status/tsdb`/`status/runtimeinfo` are sourced from a
completely different data model (the resident label cache / process start
time) than Prometheus's own head-block/process internals in the first
place, so a byte-exact comparison would not even be meaningful. Every other
fixture below (query/query_range/labels/label_values/series/metadata/
query_exemplars/buildinfo/config/flags) is fully reproducible given the
fixed seed data and image digest above.

`metadata.scrape_derived_get.json` reflects `go_goroutines`, a real,
self-scraped Go runtime metric — captured for its **envelope shape**
(`{"<name>":[{"type","help","unit"}]}`) only: PulsusDB's own `/metadata` is
sourced from `metric_metadata` (populated by remote-write/OTLP metadata
frames), not from a scrape-target cache, so the specific metric name/help
text is not meaningful to compare against — only the wire shape is.

## Fixtures and checksums

See [`SHA256SUMS`](SHA256SUMS) (regenerated by `capture.sh` on every run,
`sha256sum -c SHA256SUMS` to verify the committed files match). Re-run
`capture.sh` and update this file's capture date/image digest whenever the
fixture set needs to change.
